title: Python SDK with async snarkjs subprocess bridge

artifacts.description: Package root exporting prove_handshake, delegate, and exception classes
NodeBridge class managing Node.js subprocess lifecycle with stdin/stdout JSON framing, both sync and async variants
Pydantic v2 models: HandshakeInput, HandshakeProof, DelegateInput, DelegateProof, BridgeRequest, BridgeResponse
Structured exception hierarchy: BridgeError (base), CircuitNotFoundError, ProofVerificationError, BridgeTimeoutError
Bundled Node.js bridge process: reads JSON from stdin, calls snarkjs groth16.prove / groth16.verify, writes JSON to stdout, handles SIGTERM
Minimal package.json pinning snarkjs version for the bundled bridge
BolyraAuthTool extending LangChain BaseTool with _run/_arun wrappers for prove_handshake and delegate; installed via bolyra[langchain] extras
Package metadata, optional dependencies [langchain], entry points, Python >=3.10 constraint
Unit tests for NodeBridge using subprocess mocking; covers happy path, timeout, malformed JSON, missing circuit file
Pydantic v2 validation tests for all input/output models including field coercion and rejection cases
Integration test for BolyraAuthTool.run and arun with mocked bridge, verifying LangChain tool schema compliance
End-to-end test requiring Node.js + snarkjs installed; proves and verifies a handshake using real circuit artifacts
Quick-start guide: pip install, Node.js prerequisite, prove_handshake example, delegate example, LangChain tool usage, exception handling
Bridge wire protocol spec: JSON envelope schema (action, payload, request_id), error codes, timeout contract, versioning

steps: Define bridge wire protocol: JSON envelope {action, payload, request_id} over stdin/stdout with newline framing; document in PROTOCOL.md
Author bolyra/node_bridge/index.js: readline loop reading JSON, dispatch to snarkjs groth16.prove/verify/fullProve, write response JSON, exit cleanly on SIGTERM
Run npm install snarkjs inside node_bridge/ and commit package-lock.json; pin snarkjs to a specific minor version
Implement bolyra/exceptions.py with BridgeError base and subclasses CircuitNotFoundError, ProofVerificationError, BridgeTimeoutError mapping to bridge error codes
Implement bolyra/models.py with Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes for HandshakeInput, HandshakeProof, DelegateInput, DelegateProof, BridgeRequest, BridgeResponse
Implement bolyra/bridge.py NodeBridge: spawn Node subprocess pointing to bundled index.js, send JSON line, read JSON line, deserialize response, raise typed exceptions on error codes
Add async variant in bridge.py using asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and asyncio StreamReader for non-blocking I/O
Implement top-level bolyra/__init__.py: module-level prove_handshake(input) and delegate(input) functions that instantiate NodeBridge, call it, and return typed Pydantic models
Write pyproject.toml: hatchling build backend, optional deps [langchain] = ['langchain-core>=0.2'], include node_bridge/ as package data
Implement bolyra/langchain_tool.py: BolyraAuthTool(BaseTool) with name, description, args_schema pointing to HandshakeInput, _run calling sync prove_handshake, _arun calling async variant
Write unit tests test_bridge.py using unittest.mock.patch on subprocess.Popen; cover success, timeout (configurable), bad JSON response, non-zero exit
Write unit tests test_models.py validating Pydantic v2 field validation, alias handling, and serialization round-trips
Write test_langchain_tool.py verifying BolyraAuthTool.args_schema, tool name, and mocked _run/_arun return shapes
Write integration test test_e2e_bridge.py gated by pytest.importorskip or a CI env flag; uses real snarkjs with test .wasm/.zkey fixtures
Verify all tests pass: pytest tests/ -x -q; confirm no imports of langchain fail when extra not installed
Write README.md with pip install bolyra, Node.js >= 18 prereq note, minimal prove_handshake snippet, LangChain tool example, exception handling table
dependencies: Existing .wasm and .zkey circuit artifacts from tier2_007 for integration tests
